bids.reasoning: At t=0.0 with no offer or message history, I am opening with my maximum-utility bid, targeting utility 1.00. Since there is no evidence yet about the opponent’s priorities and no time pressure, I should not concede on any issue yet.

At t=0.02 it is still very early, so I am targeting a near-max utility bid while making only a small concession to avoid simply repeating my opening offer. The opponent’s only bid was the all-valueA extreme, which is very low utility for me and does not yet show meaningful concession, so I concede only on my lowest-weight issueA while holding firm on the high-weight issues C and D.

I am still targeting a very high-utility bid because it is extremely early (t=0.04) and the opponent has only repeated the minimum-value offer for me, showing no real concession yet. I will make a small concession only on issueA, my lowest-weight issue, while holding firm on issueB, issueC, and issueD where my utility impact is larger.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.84 since it is still very early at t=0.06 and there is no need to concede much yet. The opponent has mostly held at valueA, with only a small move on issueC to valueB, so I will keep firm on high-importance issueC and issueD while conceding further only on the lowest-weight issueA.

I am targeting a still-high utility bid by conceding only on issueA, my lowest-weight issue, while holding firm on issueB, issueC, and issueD where my utility impact is much larger. It is very early at t=0.08, and the opponent has barely conceded, only moving issueC from valueA to valueB, so there is no reason to make major concessions yet.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.82 because it is still very early, so there is little reason to concede on the high-weight issues C and D yet. The opponent has mostly held at very low-value offers for me, with only small movement on issueB/issueC, so I will concede only on my lowest-weight issueA while keeping my important issues at valueF.

At t=0.12 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid while making a modest concession to avoid simply repeating my last offer. The opponent has remained very firm on issueA/valueA and issueD/valueA, with only limited movement on issueB and issueC, so I concede on the lower-weight issueA and slightly on issueB while holding my high-importance issueC and issueD at valueF.

At t=0.14 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid while making a modest concession on lower-weight issues. The opponent has mostly held very low values for issueA/issueB/issueD but has improved issueC substantially, so I will hold firm on the high-weight issueC and issueD while offering some movement on issueB and keeping issueA low.

At t=0.16 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid while making a small, credible concession away from my maximum. The opponent’s offers remain low for me and inconsistent rather than clearly conceding, though they have shown some movement on issueC and issueD; I will hold firm on high-weight issueD while offering a modest concession on issueC and keeping issueA already fully conceded.

I am targeting a still-high utility around 0.75 because it is early in the negotiation, so I should not concede much on the high-weight issues C and D. The opponent has finally conceded issueD to my best valueF, while still holding issueB and issueC relatively low, so I keep D firm at valueF and offer a modest adjustment on low-weight issueA without repeating my last bid.

I am targeting a utility around 0.70, conceding mainly on lower-weight issueA and issueB while protecting my high-weight issueC and issueD. At t=0.20 there is still time, but the opponent has shown some movement on issueB, issueC, and once on issueD, so I am combining values they have previously signaled while avoiding repeating my last bid exactly.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around the mid/high range while making a meaningful concession because the opponent’s latest offer improved substantially for me on issueC and issueD. Since it is still early at t=0.22, I am not dropping to their last bid, but I am conceding mainly on issueA/issueB and accepting their signaled flexibility on issueC while holding firm on the highly important issueD.

I am targeting a modest concession from my last offer, around a 0.66 utility level for me, because it is still early enough at t=0.24 to protect high-weight issues while showing movement. The opponent has mostly offered low-value bids for me, but has signaled some flexibility on issueB and issueC, so I concede on lower/mid-impact areas while holding issueD at my top value.

I am targeting a solid but more conciliatory utility by keeping high value on the important issues C and D while conceding on lower-weight issueA and moderately on issueB. At t=0.26 there is still time, but the opponent has shown some willingness to offer issueC=valueD and issueD=valueE, so I am moving toward that signaled zone rather than holding issueD=valueF. Their offers are inconsistent but have improved from all valueA, especially on C/D, so this bid tests a plausible compromise without over-conceding.

I am targeting a strong utility around 0.79 because it is still early-mid negotiation at t=0.28, so I do not need to make a deep concession yet. The opponent’s recent bids show willingness to place issueB at valueF and earlier signaled issueC:valueC and issueD:valueE, so I am combining values they have already used while holding onto my high-weight issues C and D.

I am targeting a utility around 0.75: a meaningful concession from my last offer while still protecting the high-weight issues C and D. At t=0.30 there is time left, so I should not drop to the opponent’s low-utility offers, but their history shows some willingness to use issueB:valueF and the issueC:valueD / issueD:valueE combination, making this a plausible tradeoff.

I am targeting a moderate utility around 0.65, conceding mainly on lower-weight issueA and issueB while preserving decent outcomes on high-weight issueC and issueD. At t=0.32 there is no deadline panic, but the opponent has repeatedly offered issueC:valueD and once offered issueD:valueE, so this bid combines values they have signaled while still improving on their offers for me. There are no opponent messages to factor in.

I am targeting about 0.65 utility, which is appropriate for the early-mid stage at t=0.34 without conceding too deeply yet. The opponent’s offers are still mostly low for me, but they have shown some willingness on issueC and issueD, so I am holding those high-importance issues at strong values while conceding mainly on the low-weight issueA and moderately on issueB.

I am targeting a mid-utility compromise around 0.64, since it is still relatively early-mid negotiation and I should not concede too far yet. The opponent has mostly held low values for issueA and issueB but recently offered issueD: valueF, so I concede on the lower-weight A/B issues while holding strong on issueD and choosing a reasonable issueC value they have previously offered.
